name: #281 · 6f3f1151eb96f1dba9cc8f89acd025265f3a46bd · Karol Wojcik · test: add 18 snapshot ref stability tests covering determinism, structural independence, and disambiguation

newTests.name:
Comprehensive ref stability tests using controlled HTML.

   Tests that content-hash refs (FNV-1a of role|name|tag) are stable across
   DOM mutations that don't change element identity. Uses set-content! for
   fully deterministic HTML — no external site dependencies. > elements with identical role+name+tag > two buttons with same text get distinct refs

Comprehensive ref stability tests using controlled HTML.

   Tests that content-hash refs (FNV-1a of role|name|tag) are stable across
   DOM mutations that don't change element identity. Uses set-content! for
   fully deterministic HTML — no external site dependencies. > elements with identical role+name+tag > adding unrelated element between duplicates preserves both refs

Comprehensive ref stability tests using controlled HTML.

   Tests that content-hash refs (FNV-1a of role|name|tag) are stable across
   DOM mutations that don't change element identity. Uses set-content! for
   fully deterministic HTML — no external site dependencies. > identity-changing mutations > changing aria-label produces a different ref

Comprehensive ref stability tests using controlled HTML.

   Tests that content-hash refs (FNV-1a of role|name|tag) are stable across
   DOM mutations that don't change element identity. Uses set-content! for
   fully deterministic HTML — no external site dependencies. > compound mutations > simultaneous add + remove + restyle leaves target refs stable

Comprehensive ref stability tests using controlled HTML.

   Tests that content-hash refs (FNV-1a of role|name|tag) are stable across
   DOM mutations that don't change element identity. Uses set-content! for
   fully deterministic HTML — no external site dependencies. > structural wrapping > wrapping element in new container div does not change its ref

   Tests that content-hash refs (FNV-1a of role|name|tag) are stable across

   fully deterministic HTML — no external site dependencies. > identity-changing mutations > changing button text produces a different ref

   fully deterministic HTML — no external site dependencies. > visibility changes > hiding then showing element restores the same ref

Comprehensive ref stability tests using controlled HTML.

   Tests that content-hash refs (FNV-1a of role|name|tag) are stable across
   DOM mutations that don't change element identity. Uses set-content! for
   fully deterministic HTML — no external site dependencies. > adding unrelated elements > prepending a new element at beginning does not change existing refs

Comprehensive ref stability tests using controlled HTML.

   Tests that content-hash refs (FNV-1a of role|name|tag) are stable across
   DOM mutations that don't change element identity. Uses set-content! for
   fully deterministic HTML — no external site dependencies. > reordering unique elements > reversing element order preserves all refs when identities are unique

Comprehensive ref stability tests using controlled HTML.

   fully deterministic HTML — no external site dependencies. > elements with HTML id attribute > two elements with different ids get distinct stable refs

   fully deterministic HTML — no external site dependencies. > removing unrelated elements > removing one element does not change other refs
